title: Grewia bakeriana
intro: Grewia bakeriana is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, pain, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Grewia bakeriana include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Grewia bakeriana is classified within the Malvaceae family. This family is part of the order Malvales and is a family of flowering plants often referred to as the mallow or hibiscus family. The Malvaceae family is a large and diverse group, comprising over 2,300 species. The family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Grewia bakeriana is a species of flowering plant that is native to Madagascar.
